pr.title: Cleanup MObjMakeTExp with TExp constants, variable naming and simplification, and assert lines from hex to decimal
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up SysDolphin baselib MObj TEV/TExp construction code by replacing magic numeric TEV, texture, render-mode, and lightmap constants with named constants, simplifying loops and temporary variables, and converting assert_line arguments from hex to decimal. The PR also added HSD_TEXP_RAS, HSD_TEXP_TEX, and HSD_TEXP_ZERO constants to texp.h, making MObjMakeTExp substantially more readable for later TExp decompilation work.
what_changed: Added TExp source constants in include/sysdolphin/baselib/texp.h: HSD_TEXP_RAS = -2, HSD_TEXP_TEX = -1, and HSD_TEXP_ZERO = 0.

Refactored MObjMakeTExp variable names from stack/register-style placeholders such as sp3C, sp30, sp38, result, cur, cur_2, and texptev_* into semantic names such as diff, spec, ext, alpha, exp, toon, done, and tobj_*.

Replaced raw TEV and GX numeric arguments in MObjMakeTExp with named constants including GX_TEV_ADD, GX_TB_ZERO, GX_CS_SCALE_1, GX_ENABLE, GX_COLOR0A0, GX_COLOR1A1, HSD_TE_0, HSD_TE_RGB, HSD_TE_A, HSD_TE_X, HSD_TE_U8, and HSD_TE_F32.
Replaced raw texture source constants like -2, -1, 0, and NULL usages in TExp input calls with HSD_TEXP_RAS, HSD_TEXP_TEX, and HSD_TEXP_ZERO where appropriate.
Replaced raw render-mode bit tests with named render constants such as RENDER_VERTEX, RENDER_DIFFUSE, RENDER_SPECULAR, RENDER_SHADOW, and RENDER_TOON.
Replaced raw texture/lightmap flags such as 0x50, 0x20, and 0x80 with TEX_LIGHTMAP_DIFFUSE, TEX_LIGHTMAP_AMBIENT, TEX_LIGHTMAP_SPECULAR, and TEX_LIGHTMAP_EXT.
Replaced raw invalid texture map ID 0xFF with GX_TEXMAP_NULL.
Changed toon texture detection from a raw flags check against 0xF and value 4 to tobj_coord(tobj) == TEX_COORD_TOON.
Simplified list traversal from while loops with manual cursor advancement to for loops over HSD_TObj linked lists.
Renamed pointer-to-tail variables in HSD_MObjCompileTev and HSD_MObjSetup from tobj_list/var_r29 to tail and reused the tail pattern for temporarily appending shadow textures.
Converted several assert_line calls from hexadecimal line numbers to decimal line numbers, for example 0x1A0 to 416, 0x270 to 624, 0x393 to 915, 0x3AF to 943, and 0x3DE to 990.
smart_moves: Introduced HSD_TEXP_RAS, HSD_TEXP_TEX, and HSD_TEXP_ZERO at the header level instead of scattering explanatory comments or local defines, allowing later TExp work to reuse the names consistently.

Preserved the apparent TEV-building control flow while making each data path legible: diffuse, alpha, specular, and external lightmap state are tracked with semantic variables rather than decompiler temporaries.
Used existing or newly named constants to reveal the meaning of TExp calls without changing the function boundaries or algorithmic structure.

Kept the temporary shadow-texture append/remove pattern intact but renamed the tail pointer, clarifying that the code temporarily links global shadow textures into the local TObj chain and then detaches them.
Converted magic render-mode and lightmap bitmasks to named flags, making future behavior checks easier to audit against material and texture semantics.
The PR body explicitly states the motivation: MakeTExp needed constants to be understandable for later TExp function work.
decomp_lessons: When a recently matched function contains many raw GX/TEV constants, adding named constants can be a high-value cleanup before decompiling adjacent functions that depend on the same API vocabulary.
For TEV/TExp builder code, semantic accumulator names such as diff, alpha, spec, and ext communicate the pipeline better than preserving decompiler-derived stack temporary names.
Prefer named render, texture-coordinate, lightmap, and GX constants over raw bitmasks and numeric enum values; the resulting code is more reviewable and less error-prone.
Pointer-to-pointer traversal for linked-list tail insertion is easier to understand when named tail rather than var_r29 or tobj_list.
If assert_line arguments are being standardized in decimal in the surrounding codebase, convert hex line literals during cleanup without otherwise changing assertion behavior.
When replacing raw coordinate checks, a helper like tobj_coord(tobj) == TEX_COORD_TOON documents intent better than masking flags directly.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_TEXP_* for special TExp source constants: HSD_TEXP_RAS, HSD_TEXP_TEX, HSD_TEXP_ZERO.
Use HSD_TE_* constants for TExp input selectors and component/type selectors, such as HSD_TE_0, HSD_TE_RGB, HSD_TE_A, HSD_TE_X, HSD_TE_U8, HSD_TE_F32, and HSD_TE_ALL.
Use GX_* names for GameCube GX TEV constants and texture map sentinel values, such as GX_TEV_ADD, GX_TB_ZERO, GX_CS_SCALE_1, GX_ENABLE, GX_COLOR0A0, GX_COLOR1A1, and GX_TEXMAP_NULL.
Use RENDER_* names for material render-mode bits, including RENDER_VERTEX, RENDER_DIFFUSE, RENDER_SPECULAR, RENDER_SHADOW, and RENDER_TOON.
Use TEX_LIGHTMAP_* names for texture lightmap flags: TEX_LIGHTMAP_DIFFUSE, TEX_LIGHTMAP_AMBIENT, TEX_LIGHTMAP_SPECULAR, and TEX_LIGHTMAP_EXT.
Name MObjMakeTExp accumulators by material/TEV role: diff, alpha, spec, and ext.
Name linked-list append cursor tail when using an HSD_TObj** to find and later detach the end of a temporary texture object list.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR appears to be a cleanup/readability pass rather than a new matching effort; the evidence does not mention new asm matching issues.
Maintained existing control-flow shape while changing names and constants, a useful tactic for preserving a match during decomp cleanup.
Simplified cursor loops into for loops over linked lists, suggesting the compiler match tolerated more idiomatic traversal in these spots.
Kept unused stack padding as u32 unused[5], indicating the cleanup avoided removing a likely match-sensitive local stack artifact.
Replaced magic constants with preprocessor constants/enums, which should not affect generated code when values remain identical.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review and was merged the same day, but the provided excerpt does not include reviewer rationale or requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use HSD_TEXP_RAS, HSD_TEXP_TEX, or HSD_TEXP_ZERO in TExp cleanup or matching.
Check whether more raw GX TEV constants remain in src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c or related TExp files.
Look for subsequent cleanup of HSD_TExpColorIn and HSD_TExpAlphaIn call sites using the same constant naming pattern.
Verify whether assert_line decimal conversion became a broader convention across SysDolphin baselib files.
